verdict_reasoning: ATP5F1B is the catalytic beta subunit of mitochondrial F1Fo ATP synthase, canonically resident on the matrix face of the inner mitochondrial membrane. However, ectopic cell-surface expression of ATP synthase components (including beta subunit) is well documented on hepatocytes, endothelial cells, adipocytes, and tumor cells, where it functions as a receptor for apolipoproteins, angiostatin, and other ligands. This ectopic surface ATP synthase has been targeted by antibodies in preclinical oncology, qualifying as a tissue/state-restricted surface pool.
